copyright: Peggy's Point Lighthouse, Atlantic Coast, Nova Scotia, Canada (© Prashanth Bala/Shutterstock)
description: Peggy's Cove is a small yet captivating village on the rugged coast of Nova Scotia that offers a glimpse of life by the sea. The village's most famous landmark is Peggy's Point Lighthouse—also known as Peggy's Cove Lighthouse—first established in 1868 and replaced by the current structure in 1915. For over a century, it has guided ships through the ever-changing Atlantic waters, serving as a timeless symbol of coastal life. The surrounding landscape, with its windswept beauty and crashing waves, feels almost untouched by time.

copyright: African buffalo, Ngorongoro Crater, Tanzania (© jesuss8/500px/Getty Images)
description: The African buffalo, a powerful and imposing creature, roams the savannas and wetlands of Tanzania, particularly within the Ngorongoro Conservation Area (NCA). Known for their large, curved horns and massive size, they are one of Africa's 'Big Five' (buffalo, elephant, leopard, rhino, lion) and are widely respected for their strength and resilience. The NCA, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, offers a dramatic and ecologically rich setting for observing these formidable herbivores.

copyright: Radio City Music Hall in New York City, United States (© Clarence Holmes Photography/Alamy)
description: At first glance, there's no doubt that the interior of Radio City Music Hall pulls you in and cranks up the excitement. It feels like stepping straight into the sun in the middle of New York City, United States—an Art Deco fever dream of how the 1930s imagined the future. This rush of awe is celebrated every 27 March as World Theatre Day.

copyright: Logan Creek Suspension Bridge, West Coast Trail, Canada (© Tandem Stock/Adobe Stock)
description: Running along the remote southwest coast of Vancouver Island in Canada, the West Coast Trail is one of the country's most legendary hikes. Located in British Columbia's Pacific Rim National Park Reserve, the trail stretches about 75 kilometres through rainforest, beaches and rocky shoreline. It was established in 1907 as a lifesaving route for shipwreck survivors along what sailors once called the 'Graveyard of the Pacific.'

copyright: Juvenile manatees in a freshwater spring, Crystal River, Florida, United States (© Gregory Sweeney/Getty Images)
description: Manatees may seem slow and sleepy, but they have some surprising tricks under the surface. For starters, they are one of the few mammals that constantly replace their teeth. As old molars wear down from chewing gritty seagrass, new ones slowly roll forward like a conveyor belt—handy for an animal that eats for up to eight hours a day.

copyright: Cherry blossoms at East Lake Cherry Blossom Park, Wuhan, China (© Zhang Qiao/VCG/Getty Images)
description: From above, East Lake Cherry Blossom Park in Wuhan, China, looks like a soft lilac cloud settled gently over the land. Thousands of cherry trees bloom at once, their pale petals forming a living canopy around a golden five-storey pagoda at the park's heart. The pagoda rises in calm symmetry, its tiered rooflines glowing against the delicate pastels of spring.

copyright: Lightning storm over saguaro cacti, Sonoran Desert, Arizona, United States (© Jack Dykinga/Nature Picture Library)
description: The strength of nature is nowhere more evident than in powerful storms. Scary? Yes. Fascinating? Even more so. Learning to understand and respect these forces helps humanity live more safely.
